channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A daytime Russian missile attack on the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia killed at least 13 civilians and wounded about 30 others on Wednesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is facing more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power. The self-declared socialist is widely believed to have lost last year's election by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gunshots are heard near Chad's presidential palace. Foreign minister says it's under control
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — Multiple gunshots were heard near the presidential palace in Chad's capital Wednesday evening, residents said, while the African country's foreign minister said soldiers were defending the president and the situation was under control.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Americans jailed in Venezuela pose a test of Trump's deal-making foreign policy
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro will be sworn in for a third term Friday while hundreds of government opponents arrested since his disputed reelection last summer languish in the country's packed prisons. Sharing cells with them are as many as 10 Americans.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela's political newcomer Edmundo González says it's his turn to rule
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Edmundo González has become a beacon of hope for millions of Venezuelans. They want to call him president. He believes he won that office at the ballot box last year. The government of President Nicolás Maduro says he did not.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostage found dead in Gaza, military says
item.description: Israeli forces have recovered the body of a hostage held in Gaza, the military said Wednesday, adding that it was identifying additional remains that could belong to another captive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González is rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano And Astrid Suárez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian leaders say Trump's talk about Canada becoming the 51st state isn't funny anymore
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump’s comments that Canada should become the 51st state are no longer a joke and are meant to undermine America’s closest ally, Canada’s finance minister said Wednesday. Dominic LeBlanc, the country’s point person for U.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries: 'Confused' by Trump's 'obsession' with Greenland
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) hammered President-elect Trump on Wednesday for what he characterized as an “obsession” with the idea of the U.S. taking over Greenland, accusing Trump of fixating on an issue of no consequence for working-class Americans.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Gingrich: Trump 'trying to project' strength with Greenland talk
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) says President-elect Trump is attempting to project strength with his recent remarks about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark, comments that have not been well-received across the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Tristan Lejeune, The Hill

item.title: Bolton: Trump's Greenland gambit could help Xi, Putin
item.description: John Bolton, President-elect Trump's former national security adviser, said Trump’s unwillingness to rule out military force on Greenland could bolster the positions of Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin as they seek to justify their expansionist visions.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: France says EU won't tolerate attack on borders after Trump Greenland takeover remarks
item.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot says the European Union (EU) will stand firm in its alliance in the face of repeated comments from President-elect Trump about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump expected to appoint Eric Trager as Middle East director for National Security Council
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to announce Eric Trager as Senior Director for the Middle East and North Africa on the National Security Council, a congressional aide told The Hill. Trager has served as a professional staff member for Republicans on the Senate Armed Services Committee since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Italian journalist is freed from detention in Iran and returns home
item.description: An Italian journalist detained in Iran for three weeks has been freed and has returned home, after her fate had become intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer arrested in Italy and wanted by the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tents arrive for survivors of a quake that killed 126 in freezing, high-altitude Tibet
item.description: Rescuers in the freezing, high-altitude Tibet region in western China searched a second day for any remaining victims of a deadly earthquake that struck near a holy city for Tibetan Buddhists, before shifting their focus to resettling the survivors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why Greenland? Remote but resource-rich island occupies a key position in a warming world
item.description: Remote, icy and mostly pristine, Greenland plays an outsized role in the daily weather experienced by billions of people and in the climate changes taking shape all over the planet.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrants left stranded in troubled resort as Mexico disperses them far from US border
item.description: ACAPULCO, Mexico (AP) — About 100 migrants from various countries wandered directionless and disoriented through the streets of the troubled Pacific coast resort of Acapulco.
item.dc:creator: ANTONIO CASTILLO and MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France marks 10 years since deadly Charlie Hebdo attack
item.description: PARIS (AP) — A decade after Islamic extremist gunmen stormed the offices of the satirical newspaper Charlie Hebdo in a deadly assault that shook France to its core and ignited a global outcry in defense of freedom of speech, the nation paused on Tuesday to honor the victims and renew its resolve to
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earthquake 50 miles from Mount Everest leaves at least 126 dead in Tibet
item.description: A powerful magnitude 7.1 earthquake has struck Tibet, reportedly killing at least 126 on the Chinese side of the border and damaging more than 1,000 homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Law student killed by elephant during vacation to Thailand: officials
item.description: Blanca Ojanguren, a 22-year-old student from Spain, was killed by an elephant on Friday during a bathing session at Koh Yao Elephant Care
item.dc:creator: Fox News
